properties.remark: 2 TREES DOWN.
TREES DOWN DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
TREES AND WIRES WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS THE ROADWAY.
ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF OF 2 HOMES, INCLUDING A DOG KENNEL BUSINESS. TWO OTHER BUILDINGS SUSTAINED MINOR SIDING DAMAGE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN OR UPROOTED THROUGHOUT THE CITY OF ITHACA. THERE WERE ALSO SOME POWER LINES DOWN.

STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A BRIEF...EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS BETWEEN 70 TO 80 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS JUST UNDER A HALF MILE...WITH A WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. SOME TREES WERE UPROOTED AND TWO SMALL SHEDS DESTROYED.
TREES DOWN.

*** 1 INJ *** STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED SPEEDS OF 100 TO 120 MPH. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 13 MINUTES AND TRACKED FOR 6.5 MILES. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 150 YARDS. A LARGE BARN WAS DESTROYED AT THIS LOCATION...A ROOF WAS TORN OFF A HOME AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND SHEDS WERE DESTROYED. ELECTRICAL POLES WERE SNAPPED. A SECOND FARM ON UPHAM ROAD WAS HIT... DESTROYING A LARGE BARN...SILO AND SHEDS. A FARM NEAR NILES ROAD ALSO RECEIVED DAMAGE TO A SILO AND HOME.
LAW ENFORCEMENT DISPATCHERS REPORTED GEORGETOWN HIT HARD BY SEVERE THUNDERSTORM. ROOF TORN OFF HOME. OTHER STRUCTURES COLLAPSED. INJURIES REPORTED, BUT UNKNOWN NUMBER AT THIS TIME. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
ROOF BLOWN OFF MOBILE HOME. ALSO TREES AND WIRES DOWN IN TOWN OF FENTON AND TOWN AND CHENANGO IN BROOME COUNTY.

A STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE IN THIS AREA. A ROOF WAS TORN OFF AN APARTMENT BUILDING ON WYNKOOP CREEK RD. EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ON WEST DRYBROOK RD.

STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT DAMAGE FROM SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS WAS CAUSED BY A TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR STATE ROUTE 34, ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF WAVERLY. ESTIMATED SPEEDS WITHIN THIS TORNADO WERE 60 MPH GIVING THIS A RATING OF EF-0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE. THE TORNADO TRACKED NORTHEAST AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 5 MINUTES ACCORDING TO EYEWITNESS REPORTS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT ONE QUARTER MILE...AND THE WIDTH WAS ABOUT 30 YARDS.
PUBLIC SAW TORNADO TOUCH DOWN NEAR TALMADGE HILL ROAD WEST. ROOF BLOWN OFF BARN. TREES DOWN. MINOR DAMAGE TO TRAILERS IN VICINITY.

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ON WYNCOOP CREEK RD NORTH OF THE VILLAGE OF CHEMUNG. IT HAS BEEN REPORTED THAT 8 HOMES RECEIVED DAMAGE AND 1 BARN. A ROOF WAS TORN OFF ONE HOME, AND HALF THE ROOF OFF ANOTHER. OTHER STRUCTURES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE. TWO FAMILIES ARE SEEKING SHELTER. EYEWITNESSES REPORTED SEEING A FUNNEL CLOUD.
PORTION OF ROOF AND EDGING TORN OFF HOUSE. PUBLIC SAW TORNADO TOUCH DOWN. 4 TREES DOWN. NWS EMPLOYEE FOUND DAMAGE AT THE SCENE.

PORTION OF ROOF AND EDGING TORN OFF HOUSE. PUBLIC SAW TORNADO TOUCH DOWN. 4 TREES DOWN. NWS EMPLOYEE FOUND DAMAGE AT THE SCENE. NOTE - TORNADO OCCURRED NEAR CHEMUNG - TIOGA COUNTY LINE. WAS ERRONEOUSLY NOTED AS BROOME EARLIER.
ROOF BLOWN OFF MOBILE HOME. ALSO TREES AND WIRES DOWN IN TOWN OF FENTON AND TOWN AND CHENANGO IN BROOME COUNTY. TIME CHANGED FROM LST TO EDT.

HEAVY RAIN CAUSED DEBRIS TO BLOCK THE 3 LARGE DRAINAGE PIPES, WHICH THEN CAUSED WATER TO FLOW OVER BRIDGE. THE WATER WASHED AWAY THE TAR ON TOP OF THE BRIDGE/ROAD. THE WATER ALSO TRAVELED DOWN CORK HILL ROAD TOWARDS BOW BRIDGE AND CAUSED SOME MINOR ROAD EROSION AND IMPACTED HOMEOWNERS YARDS.
WATER ON ROAD OF LOWER PART OF CORK HILL ROAD. WATER DRAINED DOWN FROM UPPER PART OF ROAD INTO THIS AREA.

TREES DOWN.

THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN BEHIND A HOUSE ON JURISTA HILL ROAD. THE FIRST SIGNS OF DAMAGE WERE TO THE TOP QUARTER OF A PINE TREE WHICH WAS TWISTED AND BENT OVER THE TREE ITSELF. CONTINUING ON A NORTHEASTERLY DIRECTION...ONE LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED WITH ONE SMALLER TREE SNAPPED. ANOTHER LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED ALONG WITH A 8 FOOT BY 16 FOOT WOODEN SHED ROLLED OVER FOR 30 YARDS. MORE TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN BEFORE THE TORNADO MOVED TOWARD ACORN FIELD, DOING MORE DAMAGE TO A BARN.

THE SECOND CONFIRMED TOUCHDOWN OF THE TORNADO WAS AT THE SKYHAVEN AIRPORT IN TUNKHANNOCK. THE FIRST SIGNS OF DAMAGE WERE TO A METAL ROOF WHICH WAS PEELED AWAY FROM A FARM OUTBUILDING. PIECES OF THIS METAL ROOF WERE MOVED IN A NORTHEASTERLY DIRECTION FOR OVER 400 YARDS. LIMITED DAMAGE TO TREES...MAINLY CONSISTING OF DOWNED BRANCHES, MOST OF WHICH WERE SMALL.

WATER OUT OF THE BANKS OF THE LITTLE BEAVERKILL RIVER IN THE TOWN OF LIVINGSTON MANOR ON OLD LIBERTY ROAD. WATER ABOUT 3 FEET HIGH IN ONE HOME AND COVERING PART OF OLD LIBERTY RD. WATER JUST BELOW THE BOTTOM OF THE BRIDGE ACROSS THE LITTLE BEAVERKILL AT THE INTERSECTION OF OLD LIBERTY RD. AND CROSS RD.

ALONG STATE ROUTE 52 JUST EAST OF YOUNGSVILLE...THE EAST BRANCH OF THE CALLICOON CREEK CAME OUT OF ITS BANK AT MOWERS AND MORE INC. WATER FLOODED THE BUSINESS AND DISPLACED NUMEROUS TREES ON EITHER SIDE OF THE CREEK. TWO LARGE TREES WERE MOVED TOWARD THE BACK OF AN ADJACENT HOUSE.
STATE ROUTE 52 CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. EVACUATIONS TAKING PLACE ALONG CALLICOON CREEK.
EVACUATIONS TAKING PLACE ALONG CALLICOON CREEK. STATE ROUTE 52 CLOSED.

ORIGINAL REPORT FROM THE EM STATED THAT EVACUATIONS TOOK PLACE ALONG CALLICOON CREEK AND STATE ROUTE 52 WAS CLOSED. AN NWS SURVEY ON AUGUST 3RD CONFIRMED THAT STATE ROUTE 52 WAS COVERED WITH WATER FROM NEAR JEFFERSONVILLE NORTH BRANCH ROAD TO KOHLERTOWN. MAJOR EROSION OCCURED ALONG THE SIDES OF JEFFERSONVILLE NORTH BRANCH ROAD DUE TO A BROOK OVERTOPPING THE ROAD. 50 TO 60 HOMES HAD TO BE PUMPED OUT IN JEFFERSONVILLE. FLOODING ALSO OCCURED IN THE ISLAND AREA...AND NEAR DEWITT FLATS RD.
ONE HOUSE SURROUNDED BY WATER. MANY ROADS CLOSED THROUGHOUT WYOMING COUNTY.
THE LEE ROAD BRIDGE OVER DUNDAFF CREEK IS WASHED OUT. ROUTE 106 IS CLOSED. BASEMENTS ARE FLOODED IN DALTON, CLARKS SUMMIT AND GREENFIELD TOWNSHIP.
ROUTE 106 IS CLOSED AND SEVERAL BUSINESSES ARE FLOODED.
FLOODING IS OCCURING IN THE JEFFERSONVILLE AREA. SEVERAL HOMES ARE BEING EVACUATED. ROUTE 164 IS FLOODED.

A COUNTY OFFICIAL ORIGINALLY REPORTED THAT SEVERAL HOMES ARE BEING EVACUATED AND ROUTE 164 IS FLOODED. A NWS SURVEY CONFIMRED THAT STATE ROUTE 164 WAS COVERED WITH WATER DUE TO A BROOK THAT OVERTOPPED THE ROAD. THIS BROOK IS A TRIBUTARY TO THE EAST BRANCH OF CALLICOON CREEK AND ORIGINATES NORTHWEST OF THE CENTRAL SCHOOL. HOUSES WERE ALSO FLOODED NEAR KOHLERTOWN DUE TO A COMBINATION OF FLOODING FROM THE BROOK MENTIONED ABOVE ALONG WITH THE EAST BRANCH OF THE CALLICOON CREEK RUNNING OVER ITS BANKS
FLOODING OCCURRED ON ROUTE 164 AND STATE ROUTE 52 IN JEFFERSONVILLE.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN BINGHAMTON, NY HAS CONFIRMED A TORNADO NEAR KIRKWOOD IN BROOME COUNTY NEW YORK ON 8/2/2009. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN AT THE BOTTOM A HILL BEHIND A STRAND OF TREES...IN WHICH THREE WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED WITH LARGE LIMBS 2 TO 5 INCHES IN DIAMETER AND AVERAGING AROUND 15 FEET LONG. TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 175 YARDS AND THEN LIFTED.
